articles.title: Kenyans mark two years since Gen Z protests
articles.summary: Two years on, families of those killed during Kenya’s 2024 anti-government protests returned to Parliament to remember their loved ones and demand justice.
articles.context: The 2024 anti-government protests in Kenya were sparked by widespread discontent over rising living costs and allegations of electoral fraud following the contentious 2023 presidential elections. The protests, which escalated into violent clashes, resulted in the deaths of over 100 individuals, prompting calls for accountability from civil society and opposition leaders like Raila Odinga. As the country approaches the 2025 elections, the demand for justice remains a critical issue in the political landscape.

articles.title: NCA adjusts telecom tariff exchange rate to avert network collapse
articles.summary: South Sudan’s National Communications Authority (NCA) has approved a phased adjustment to the telecommunications tariff The post NCA adjusts telecom tariff exchange rate to avert network collapse appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing economic challenges since its independence in 2011, exacerbated by civil conflict and hyperinflation. The NCA's decision to adjust telecom tariffs comes amid a critical need to stabilize the telecommunications sector, which has been struggling due to currency devaluation and operational costs. This adjustment follows the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, which aimed to foster peace and economic recovery in the war-torn nation.

articles.title: Governor Gai leads peace drive to end Twic East–Duk violence
articles.summary: The Governor of Jonglei State, Riek Gai Kok, has launched a peace mission to Twic East and Duk counties aimed at reconciling communities affected by recurring violence linked to disputes over fishing grounds and islands along the Nile River. The initiative follows recent clashes between the Ayual community of Twic East County and the Hol […] The post Governor Gai leads peace drive to end Twic East–Duk violence appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The ongoing violence between the Ayual community of Twic East and the Hol community of Duk has roots in longstanding disputes over fishing rights and territorial claims along the Nile River. Tensions escalated following clashes in early September 2023, resulting in casualties and displacement. Governor Riek Gai Kok's peace initiative is crucial as it seeks to address these grievances and foster reconciliation amid a fragile security situation exacerbated by political rivalries in South Sudan.

articles.title: محمد صلاح يتربع على عرش هدافى مصر فى المونديال قبل لقاء إيران.. إنفوجراف
articles.summary: واصل النجم المصري محمد صلاح كتابة التاريخ في كأس العالم، بعدما سجل هدفه الثالث في تاريخ مشاركاته المونديالية، ليعتلي صدارة هدافي منتخب مصر عبر العصور فى البطولة العالمية، عقب زيارة شباك نيوزيلندا فى الجولة الثانية من دور المجموعات لمونديال 2026، والتى انتهت بفوز منتخب مصر بنتيجة 3 – 1. وجاء هدف محمد صلاح الثاني في …
articles.context: تأتي إنجازات محمد صلاح في كأس العالم في وقت حساس لمصر، حيث يسعى المنتخب الوطني لتعزيز مكانته في الساحة الرياضية العالمية بعد سنوات من التراجع. في ظل التوترات السياسية والاقتصادية في المنطقة، يُعتبر نجاح صلاح رمزًا للأمل والوحدة، خاصة بعد تأهل مصر إلى مونديال 2026، مما يعكس تطورًا إيجابيًا في الأداء الرياضي بعد غياب عن البطولة منذ 2018.

articles.title: تكريم ياسمين عبد العزيز وأحمد سعد فى كاليفورنيا
articles.summary: كُرمت الفنانة ياسمين عبد العزيز والمطرب أحمد سعد، خلال احتفالية في مدينة Beverly Hills بولاية California اليوم الخميس، تقديرًا لمجمل أعمالهما الفنية ومسيرتهما الحافلة بالنجاحات الفنية الأخيرة تكريم ياسمين عبد العزيز وأحمد سعد فى كاليفورنيا والتقطت ياسمين برفقة أحمد سعد عدد من الصور التذكارية على هامش حفل التكريم، الذى يأتي بعد نجاحات حققتها ياسمين خاصة …
articles.context: تأتي احتفالية تكريم ياسمين عبد العزيز وأحمد سعد في كاليفورنيا في وقت تشهد فيه المنطقة توترات سياسية واقتصادية، خاصة في السودان حيث يعاني الشعب من صراعات داخلية منذ اندلاع النزاع في أبريل 2023. يعكس هذا التكريم أهمية الفنون والثقافة كوسيلة للتواصل والتعبير عن الهوية في ظل الظروف الصعبة، ويعزز من الروابط الثقافية بين الفنانين العرب والمجتمع الدولي.

articles.title: أحمد حلمى يضع اللمسات الأخيرة على أضعف خلقه قبل تحديد موعد عرضه
articles.summary: انتهى صناع فيلم أضعف خلقه بطولة النجم أحمد حلمى وتشاركه البطولة النجمة هند صبرى، من مراحل مونتاج ومكساج الفيلم الأيام الماضية، حيث استغرقت عمليات المونتاج ما يقرب من شهر حتى انتهت، وسلم مخرج العمل النسخة النهائية للشركة المنتجة استعدادًا لطرحه في دور العرض الفترة المقبلة، وطرحت الشركة المنتجة موعد مبدئى للفيلم خلال اواخر اغسطس، ولم …
articles.context: تأتي أخبار فيلم "أضعف خلقه" في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تحديات كبيرة، بما في ذلك الصراع المستمر بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع منذ أبريل 2023. هذا الصراع أدى إلى تدهور الأوضاع الإنسانية والثقافية في البلاد، مما يجعل إنتاج الأفلام والفنون أكثر أهمية كوسيلة للتعبير عن الهوية والتحديات. كما يعكس الفيلم جهود الفنانين مثل أحمد حلمى وهند صبرى في تعزيز الثقافة والفن في ظل الظروف الصعبة.

articles.title: أحمد فهمى يعود للكوميديا فى رمضان 2027 بعد ابتعاده عنها فى سره الباتع
articles.summary: يستعد النجم أحمد فهمى للعودة إلى أجواء الكوميديا خلال موسم دراما رمضان 2027، بعدما تعاقد رسميًا على بطولة مسلسل كوميدي جديد من إنتاج شركة سينرجي للمنتج تامر مرسي، في تعاون يجمعه بالمؤلف شريف الليثي والمخرج معتز التوني، وذلك ابتعد عن الكوميديا في آخر أعماله بدراما رمضان، مسلسل سره الباتع. سره الباتع آخر أعمال أحمد فهمي …
articles.context: تأتي عودة أحمد فهمي للكوميديا في رمضان 2027 في وقت يشهد فيه السودان تحديات اقتصادية وسياسية كبيرة، حيث تتصاعد الأزمات الإنسانية والنزاعات المسلحة. في ظل هذه الظروف، تلعب الدراما والكوميديا دورًا مهمًا في تخفيف الضغوط النفسية وتقديم رسائل اجتماعية. كما أن التعاون مع شركة سينرجي يعكس جهود القطاع الفني في تعزيز الإنتاج المحلي وسط الأزمات.

articles.title: ميرنا جميل تحتفل بعيد ميلادها.. رحلة من الأردن لقلوب المصريين
articles.summary: تحتفل الفنانة ميرنا جميل، اليوم الخميس، بعيد ميلادها، بعد مسيرة فنية قصيرة لكنها حافلة بالأدوار اللي تركت علامة عند الجمهور، خاصة في الكوميديا. من الأردن لمسرح خالد جلال ولدت ميرنا جميل في 25 يونيو 1992 بالأردن، لكن جمالها وموهبتها كانا السبب الرئيسي في دخولها عالم التمثيل بمصر، وكانت بدايتها الحقيقية على خشبة المسرح مع المخرج …
articles.context: تأتي احتفالية ميرنا جميل بعيد ميلادها في وقت تشهد فيه المنطقة تحولات ثقافية واجتماعية كبيرة، خاصة مع تزايد التعاون الفني بين مصر والأردن. بعد اتفاقية السلام الأردنية المصرية في عام 1979، أصبحت الفنون وسيلة لتعزيز العلاقات بين البلدين، مما يسهم في تعزيز الهوية الثقافية المشتركة. في ظل الأوضاع السياسية الحالية، تُعتبر قصص النجاح مثل قصة ميرنا جميل رمزًا للأمل والتواصل بين الشعوب.

articles.title: Kapuri bridge at risk of structural failure, ministry orders immediate traffic restrictions
articles.summary: The Ministry of Roads and Bridges has announced the immediate enforcement of a vehicle load restriction on Kapuri Bridge along the Juba, Luri, Mundri road corridor, citing serious structural deterioration that poses a threat to public safety. In an official order issued in Juba today (25 June 2026), the Ministry said a recent technical inspection […] The post Kapuri bridge at risk of structural failure, ministry orders immediate traffic restrictions appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The Kapuri Bridge is a critical infrastructure link in South Sudan, facilitating trade and movement between Juba and the western regions, including Mundri. The bridge's deterioration comes amid ongoing tensions following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement, which aimed to stabilize the region after years of civil conflict. With the economy still fragile, any disruption in transport routes could exacerbate humanitarian challenges, particularly as the country approaches the 2024 elections.

articles.title: Defence seeks dismissal of assault case against police officer

articles.context: The case against Police Captain Richard Ruai Kuol comes amid ongoing tensions in South Sudan, where security forces have faced scrutiny for their role in violence and human rights abuses. Following the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at ending years of civil conflict, issues of accountability within the police and military remain critical, as the government struggles to maintain order and rebuild trust among citizens. The outcome of this case could influence perceptions of justice in the country.

articles.title: Speaker’s office withdraws official portrait directive following media concerns
articles.summary: The Office of the Speaker of the Transitional National Legislative Assembly (TNLA) has withdrawn a directive requiring media houses to use official portraits of Speaker Joseph Ngere Paciko, following concerns raised by media stakeholders over editorial independence and press freedom. In a statement issued on Thursday, the Speaker’s Office said the portraits had been circulated […] The post Speaker’s office withdraws official portrait directive following media concerns appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The withdrawal of the directive comes amid ongoing tensions in South Sudan's political landscape, particularly following the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, which aimed to establish a transitional government. Media freedom has been a contentious issue, with the government often accused of suppressing dissent. The Speaker's directive raised alarms among journalists, reflecting broader concerns about press independence in a fragile democracy still recovering from years of civil war.

articles.title: Abyei launches subsidized sorghum sales program to reduce rising food prices for households
articles.summary: The government of Abyei Special Administrative Area has launched a subsidized sorghum sales program to help reduce rising food prices and improve access to affordable food for households. Speaking to Eye Radio this morning, Abyei Minister of Information Yohana Akol said the initiative aims to ease the cost of living and strengthen food security during […] The post Abyei launches subsidized sorghum sales program to reduce rising food prices for households appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The Abyei region, contested between Sudan and South Sudan, has faced ongoing instability since the 2011 South Sudanese independence referendum. The Abyei Protocol of 2005 aimed to resolve the area's status, but tensions have persisted, exacerbated by economic challenges and food insecurity. Amidst this backdrop, the Abyei Special Administrative Area's new subsidized sorghum program is a critical response to rising food prices and aims to alleviate the hardships faced by local households.

articles.title: Phone and internet charges to rise as NCA approves new telecom tariffs
articles.summary: Consumers will pay more for phone calls and internet services starting Friday after the National Communication Authority (NCA) approved a phased adjustment of telecommunications tariffs. The new rates will take effect at midnight on June 26 following consultations involving the Ministry of ICT and Postal Services, Parliament, mobile network operators, and civil society organizations. According […] The post Phone and internet charges to rise as NCA approves new telecom tariffs appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The approval of new telecom tariffs by the National Communication Authority comes amid ongoing economic challenges in South Sudan, exacerbated by the 2013 civil war and subsequent peace agreements, including the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict. Rising costs for communication services could further strain citizens already facing inflation and limited access to essential services, highlighting the fragile state of the country's recovery and governance.

articles.title: In remote Akobo, a young entrepreneur turns coffee into opportunity
articles.summary: On the banks of the Pibor River in South Sudan’s remote Akobo County, the aroma The post In remote Akobo, a young entrepreneur turns coffee into opportunity appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: Akobo County, historically affected by South Sudan's civil conflict, has faced significant challenges since the outbreak of violence in December 2013, leading to widespread displacement and economic instability. The revitalization of local agriculture, particularly coffee production, is crucial for rebuilding livelihoods and fostering peace in the region. Recent peace agreements, such as the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS) signed in September 2018, aim to stabilize the area and encourage entrepreneurship.

articles.title: Civil Aviation Authority inspects newly completed Leer Airstrip
articles.summary: A delegation of senior officials from the South Sudan Civil Aviation Authority has conducted an inspection visit to the newly completed Leer Airstrip in Leer County of Unity State to assess its readiness for aviation operations. The team was led by the Director of Air Transport and Economic Regulation, alongside senior officials appointed by the […] The post Civil Aviation Authority inspects newly completed Leer Airstrip appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The inspection of the Leer Airstrip comes amid ongoing efforts to improve infrastructure in South Sudan, a country grappling with the aftermath of a civil war that began in 2013. The revitalization of air transport is crucial for economic recovery and connectivity, particularly following the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, which aimed to foster peace and stability. Enhanced aviation capabilities could facilitate humanitarian aid and promote regional trade.

articles.title: Lam Akol: Don’t blame me for South Sudan’s failures, I served only seven months in gov’t
articles.summary: Veteran opposition leader Dr. Lam Akol Ajawin says he should not be blamed for governance failures in South Sudan, arguing that he has spent only seven months in government since 2005. Dr. Lam, who leads the National Democratic Movement (NDM), said it would be unfair to hold him responsible for the country’s challenges when he […] The post Lam Akol: Don’t blame me for South Sudan’s failures, I served only seven months in gov’t appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: Dr. Lam Akol Ajawin's comments come amid ongoing political instability in South Sudan, exacerbated by the 2013 civil war and the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS). His party, the National Democratic Movement, is part of a fragile coalition government formed in February 2020, which has struggled to implement key provisions, including security arrangements and governance reforms, raising questions about accountability among political leaders.

articles.title: Government sets proposed date for inter-party dialogue launch ahead of elections
articles.summary: The Minister of Cabinet Affairs says a date has been proposed for the launch of the Inter-Party Dialogue aimed at building consensus among political stakeholders ahead of elections. The announcement by Minister Dr. Martin Elia Lomuro, who also serves as rapporteur of the Inter-Party Steering Committee, comes after the Office of the Senior Presidential Advisor […] The post Government sets proposed date for inter-party dialogue launch ahead of elections appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing political instability since its independence in 2011, exacerbated by a civil war that erupted in 2013. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish a transitional government and promote peace, but tensions among political factions, including the SPLM-IO led by Riek Machar and the SPLM under President Salva Kiir, persist. The proposed inter-party dialogue is crucial for fostering unity ahead of the upcoming elections scheduled for 2024.

articles.title: Campaigners call for end to conflict-related sexual violence in South Sudan
articles.summary: Human rights campaigners and authorities have called for an end to conflict-related sexual violence in South Sudan, saying urgent action is needed to protect survivors and ensure justice. The call was made during a two-day event in Juba organized by the Center for Inclusive Governance, Peace, and Justice (CIGP), aimed at strengthening advocacy against conflict-related […] The post Campaigners call for end to conflict-related sexual violence in South Sudan appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: South Sudan has been embroiled in civil conflict since its independence from Sudan in 2011, with violence escalating after the outbreak of a power struggle between President Salva Kiir and former Vice President Riek Machar in 2013. Despite the 2018 Revitalized Peace Agreement aimed at ending hostilities, reports of sexual violence as a weapon of war persist, highlighting the urgent need for accountability and support for survivors amidst ongoing instability and political fragmentation.

articles.title: Groundwork laid for inter-party dialogue – Lomuro
articles.summary: Preparations are progressing through technical committees tasked with shaping the framework for an inter-party dialogue The post Groundwork laid for inter-party dialogue – Lomuro appeared first on Radio Tamazuj .
articles.context: The call for inter-party dialogue in South Sudan comes amid ongoing tensions following the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (R-ARCSS), which aimed to end years of civil war. Key political figures, including Vice President James Lomuro, are now pushing for dialogue to address persistent divisions among parties, particularly between President Salva Kiir's SPLM and opposition factions led by Riek Machar, as the country faces economic challenges and insecurity.

articles.title: Yei commissioner calls for more Ebola preparedness support
articles.summary: The Commissioner of Yei River County has called for increased support from health partners to help the County Health Department prepare for possible Ebola threats linked to outbreaks in neighboring Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo. Commissioner Samuel Henry Malimbo made the appeal during a one-day briefing that brought together health partners and county […] The post Yei commissioner calls for more Ebola preparedness support appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: Yei River County, located in South Sudan, has faced ongoing instability due to conflict and violence since the South Sudanese Civil War began in 2013. The recent Ebola outbreaks in Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo, particularly the 2022 outbreak in Uganda that resulted in 142 confirmed cases, have heightened concerns about cross-border health threats. This context underscores the urgency of Commissioner Samuel Henry Malimbo's call for enhanced health preparedness in Yei.

articles.title: Families honor victims as Kenya marks anniversary of deadly youth-led protests
articles.summary: Commemorations coincide with fresh protests, arrests in Nairobi
articles.context: The anniversary of the youth-led protests in Kenya, which erupted in 2020 following the police killing of George Floyd-inspired protests, highlights ongoing tensions between the government and citizens over police brutality and governance. The recent resurgence of protests in Nairobi, marked by arrests, reflects the discontent with President William Ruto's administration and its failure to address systemic issues, including corruption and economic hardship, exacerbated by the fallout from the COVID-19 pandemic.

articles.title: طلاب سودانيون يعودون إلى معهد النور للمكفوفين برغم الدمار
articles.summary: تفاقمت معاناة المكفوفين في السودان منذ اندلاع الحرب بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، بعدما تعرض المعهد الوحيد الذي يقدم لهم الخدمات التعليمية لدمار واسع.
articles.context: تأثرت أوضاع المكفوفين في السودان بشكل كبير منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تدمير معهد النور للمكفوفين في الخرطوم. يمثل هذا المعهد المؤسسة التعليمية الوحيدة التي تقدم خدماتها لهذه الفئة، مما يزيد من معاناتهم في ظل غياب الدعم الحكومي والموارد اللازمة. تبرز هذه القصة أهمية التعليم الشامل في أوقات الأزمات وتسلط الضوء على التحديات التي تواجه ذوي الاحتياجات الخاصة.

articles.title: سيناريوهات تأهل منتخب مصر إلى دور الـ32 لبطولة كأس العالم

articles.context: تأتي هذه المقالة في وقت حساس حيث يواجه منتخب مصر تحديات كبيرة في التصفيات المؤهلة لكأس العالم 2026، خاصة بعد الأداء المتذبذب في التصفيات السابقة. كما أن الوضع السياسي في المنطقة، بما في ذلك النزاع المستمر في السودان وتأثيره على العلاقات الرياضية بين الدول، يضيف بعدًا إضافيًا لأهمية تأهل مصر، حيث يعكس نجاحها في البطولة قدرة البلاد على تجاوز الأزمات وتحقيق الوحدة الوطنية.

articles.title: Northern Bahr el Ghazal launches 2,500-acre rice cultivation at Aweil scheme

articles.title: الإسماعيلى يعتمد التشكيل الجديد لقطاع الناشئين استعدادًا للموسم المقبل
articles.summary: اعتمد النادي الإسماعيلي التشكيل الجديد لقطاع الناشئين، في إطار خطة إعادة تنظيم القطاع والاستعداد للموسم الجديد، على أن تنطلق فترة الإعداد الرسمية لفرق الناشئين يوم السبت المقبل الموافق 27 يونيو. وجاء التشكيل الجديد لقطاع الناشئين على النحو التالي: الكابتن إسماعيل حفني مشرفًا عامًا على القطاع الكابتن خالد القماش رئيسًا لقطاع الناشئين الكابتن إسماعيل توتو مديرًا …

articles.summary: حصل ثابيلو ماسيكو لاعب منتخب جنوب أفريقيا على جائزة رجل مباراة فريقه أمام كوريا الجنوبيا التي جمعتهما فجر اليوم الخميس وحجز منتخب جنوب أفريقيا مقعده في دور الـ32 من بطولة كأس العالم 2026، بعدما حقق فوزًا ثمينًا على كوريا الجنوبية بهدف دون رد، في المباراة التي جمعتهما فجر اليوم الخميس على ملعب مونتيري ستاديوم، ضمن …
articles.context: تأتي هذه المباراة في سياق تصاعد الاهتمام بكأس العالم 2026، حيث يسعى منتخب جنوب أفريقيا لتعزيز مكانته في الساحة الرياضية العالمية بعد غيابه عن البطولة منذ عام 2010. كما تعكس هذه النتائج التنافس المتزايد بين الفرق الأفريقية، خاصة في ظل الدعم المتزايد من الاتحادات الرياضية المحلية لتعزيز الأداء في البطولات الدولية.

articles.title: في مثل هذا اليوم.. الزمالك ينسحب أمام الأهلي بالقمة 77 بعد هدف حسام حسن
articles.summary: في مثل هذا اليوم، 25 يونيو 1996، شهدت الكرة المصرية واحدة من أكثر مباريات القمة إثارة للجدل، بعدما انسحب الزمالك من مواجهة غريمه التقليدي الأهلي في القمة رقم 77، اعتراضًا على احتساب الهدف الثاني للفريق الأحمر. وأقيمت المباراة في ختام الأسبوع قبل الأخير من بطولة الدوري المصري، وسط صراع محتدم على اللقب، حيث دخل الفريقان …
articles.context: تأتي ذكرى انسحاب الزمالك أمام الأهلي في القمة 77 في ظل توترات مستمرة بين الأندية المصرية، حيث لا تزال المنافسة على اللقب تعكس الانقسامات السياسية والاجتماعية في البلاد. في السنوات الأخيرة، شهدت الكرة المصرية تصاعدًا في العنف بين الجماهير، مما يزيد من أهمية هذه الذكرى كدليل على كيفية تأثير الرياضة على الهوية الوطنية. كما أن الأحداث السياسية في مصر، مثل الانتخابات الأخيرة في 2023، قد أثرت أيضًا على الأجواء الرياضية.

articles.title: ارتفاع ضغط الدم.. كيف تكتشفه مبكرا وما طرق السيطرة عليه
articles.summary: ارتفاع ضغط الدم أحد أكثر الأمراض شيوعًا وخطورة في العالم، يطلق عليه الأطباء “القاتل الصامت” بسبب قدرته على التسبب في أضرار جسيمة للجسم دون ظهور أعراض واضحة في كثير من الأحيان. ما هو ارتفاع ضغط الدم؟ وفقا لموقع top doctors فإن ضغط الدم هو القوة التي يضخ بها القلب الدم عبر الشرايين إلى مختلف أعضاء …
articles.context: في ظل الأزمات الصحية المتزايدة في السودان، بما في ذلك النزاعات المستمرة والأوضاع الاقتصادية الصعبة، يواجه المواطنون تحديات كبيرة في الوصول إلى الرعاية الصحية الأساسية. ارتفاع ضغط الدم، الذي يُعتبر "القاتل الصامت"، يزداد انتشارًا في ظل هذه الظروف، مما يستدعي الحاجة الملحة للتوعية والوقاية. كما أن اتفاق السلام الموقع في 2020 لم يُحقق الاستقرار الكامل، مما يزيد من الضغوط الصحية على السكان.

articles.title: جوجل تتيح الانضمام لاجتماعات «Meet» على أجهزة iOS دون تسجيل حساب
articles.summary: أعلنت جوجل عن تحديث جديد يسهّل على مستخدمي هواتف آيفون الانضمام إلى اجتماعات Google Meet، إذ لم يعد من الضروري تثبيت تطبيق Google Meet أو Gmail للمشاركة في الاجتماعات. وبموجب التحديث الجديد، يمكن لمستخدمي أجهزة iPhone وiPad الانضمام مباشرة إلى الاجتماعات عبر متصفح Safari بمجرد الضغط على رابط الدعوة، حيث يتم توجيههم تلقائياً إلى المتصفح …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الخطوة من جوجل في وقت حرج حيث يعاني العديد من سكان إثيوبيا والسودان من انقطاع خدمات الإنترنت بسبب النزاعات المستمرة. في إثيوبيا، تستمر الصراعات في تيغراي، بينما في السودان، يشهد البلد توترات سياسية بعد الإطاحة بنظام البشير في 2019. تسهيل الوصول إلى أدوات التواصل مثل Google Meet يمكن أن يسهم في تعزيز الحوار والتعاون بين المجتمعات المحلية والدولية في ظل هذه الظروف الصعبة.

articles.title: كيف تغير ميزة إعادة ترتيب الشبكة طريقة عرض حسابك على إنستجرام؟
articles.summary: أعلن تطبيق إنستجرام عن إطلاق ميزة جديدة تتيح للمستخدمين إعادة ترتيب المنشورات داخل الملف الشخصي وفقًا لتفضيلاتهم، في خطوة تمنحهم مزيدًا من التحكم في طريقة عرض المحتوى على حساباتهم. وتنهي الميزة الجديدة أحد أبرز القيود التي رافقت التطبيق لسنوات، حيث كانت المنشورات تظهر داخل الصفحة الشخصية وفقًا للتسلسل الزمني لنشرها، مع إمكانية تثبيت عدد محدود …
articles.context: تأتي ميزة إعادة ترتيب المنشورات في إنستجرام في وقت حساس للسودان، حيث يشهد البلاد توترات سياسية متزايدة منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. في ظل هذه الظروف، يسعى العديد من المستخدمين إلى استخدام منصات التواصل الاجتماعي كوسيلة للتعبير عن آرائهم ومشاركة تجاربهم، مما يجعل القدرة على تنظيم المحتوى بشكل شخصي أكثر أهمية لتعزيز التواصل الفعّال.

articles.title: Over 100 S. Sudanese detained in Libya, 55 held in Kenya, says Speaker
articles.summary: The Speaker of the National Legislative Assembly says more than 100 South Sudanese are being detained in Libya, while 55 others are in custody in Kenya. Speaking in parliament on Tuesday, Joseph Ngere Paciko said a delegation led by lawmaker Mojur Babour recently visited Benghazi and found over 100 South Sudanese in detention. He said […] The post Over 100 S. Sudanese detained in Libya, 55 held in Kenya, says Speaker appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in South Sudan, which erupted in civil war in December 2013, has led to widespread displacement and migration, with many seeking refuge in neighboring countries. The recent visit by lawmaker Mojur Babour to Libya highlights the precarious situation for South Sudanese migrants, particularly in light of the 2017 Khartoum Agreement aimed at addressing the plight of migrants in the region. The detention of South Sudanese in Libya and Kenya underscores the urgent need for regional cooperation on migration issues.

articles.title: Jiye, Toposa reach agreement to curb conflict along border areas
articles.summary: The Jiye and Toposa communities living along the border between Eastern Equatoria State and the Greater Pibor Administrative Area have agreed on a series of measures, including the revival of traditional conflict-resolution mechanisms, to reduce conflict and strengthen peaceful coexistence following a reconciliation meeting in Wokobu kraals on Wednesday. The meeting was led by Kapoeta […] The post Jiye, Toposa reach agreement to curb conflict along border areas appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The Jiye and Toposa communities have a history of conflict over resources and territorial disputes, exacerbated by the broader instability in South Sudan since its independence in 2011. The reconciliation meeting on October 18, 2023, in Wokobu kraals is significant as it marks a renewed effort to implement the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, which emphasizes local peacebuilding initiatives to address intercommunal violence.

articles.title: دراسة: حالات الإفلاس في ألمانيا تسجل أعلى مستوى منذ 2013
articles.summary: أفادت شركة “كريديت ريفورم” للائتمان بأن حالات الإفلاس بين الشركات في ألمانيا سجلت في النصف الأول من العام الجاري أعلى مستوى لها منذ عام 2013 بواقع 13 ألف حالة. وجاء في بيان للشركة: “شهد النصف الأول من عام 2026 ارتفاعا في حالات الإفلاس بين الشركات بنسبة 7.8% مقارنة بالفترة نفسها من العام الماضي، ليصل العدد …
articles.context: تأتي هذه الزيادة في حالات الإفلاس في ألمانيا في وقت حساس حيث تتأثر الاقتصاديات العالمية بتداعيات الحرب في أوكرانيا، مما يزيد من الضغوط على الشركات الأوروبية. كما أن ارتفاع أسعار الطاقة والتضخم المستمر يساهمان في تفاقم الأوضاع الاقتصادية، مما ينعكس سلبًا على استقرار الأعمال. في هذا السياق، تعتبر هذه الأرقام مؤشرًا على التحديات التي تواجهها الشركات في ألمانيا، والتي قد تؤثر على العلاقات التجارية مع دول مثل السودان.

articles.title: 215 civilians die in RSF-run prison in South Darfur: Sudanese medics
articles.summary: Sudan Doctors Network reports spread of diseases and epidemics inside Daqris Prison
articles.context: The reported deaths in Daqris Prison occur amidst the ongoing conflict in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 when clashes broke out between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF). This power struggle has led to widespread violence and humanitarian crises, exacerbating conditions in detention facilities. The Sudan Doctors Network's report highlights the dire health situation in prisons, reflecting the broader implications of the conflict on civilian safety and human rights.

articles.title: WHO warns about worsening humanitarian crisis, cholera outbreak in Sudan's Kordofan region
articles.summary: More than 700 suspected cases, 105 deaths reported in West Kordofan since mid-May, according to authorities
articles.context: The Kordofan region of Sudan has been plagued by conflict since the outbreak of the Sudanese Civil War in 2011, which has exacerbated humanitarian crises. The ongoing violence between government forces and various rebel groups, particularly in West Kordofan, has hindered access to healthcare and sanitation. The recent escalation of cholera cases comes amid a fragile ceasefire agreement signed in July 2023, highlighting the urgent need for humanitarian assistance in a region already devastated by war.

articles.title: Central Bank’s FX Auction Sees Average rate Settles at 157 Birr per Dollar
articles.summary: The average rate of Ethiopian Birr (ETB) has slightly strengthened against the US dollar in the foreign exchange trading on Tuesday, the results released by the Central Bank
articles.context: Ethiopia's economy has faced significant challenges due to the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region, which began in November 2020, leading to disruptions in trade and investment. The government has been implementing reforms, including the liberalization of the foreign exchange market, to stabilize the economy. The recent auction results reflect these efforts, as the Central Bank aims to manage the depreciation of the Birr while fostering investor confidence amid a complex political landscape.

articles.title: Explainer: Critical constraints risk further Ebola spread as cases surpass 1,000
articles.summary: ADDIS ABABA – As the Bundibugyo Ebola virus outbreak continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, with cases already surpassing 1,000 and
articles.context: The ongoing Ebola outbreak is exacerbated by the fragile health infrastructure in the DRC and Uganda, where decades of conflict have weakened public health systems. The 2019-2020 Ebola outbreak in DRC highlighted these vulnerabilities, and the recent resurgence has raised alarms among regional health authorities. Additionally, the 2021 Kampala Declaration aimed to strengthen regional cooperation against health threats, but implementation has been inconsistent, leaving communities at risk as cases surge past 1,000.

articles.title: Human Rights Watch Rebukes Repressive Legislation in Tigray
articles.summary: Human Rights Watch has called on regional authorities in Tigray to immediately withdraw a piece of legislation that political observers have described as a “blueprint for authoritarianism.” The international watchdog issued the call after the Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) introduced a sweeping security and mobilization proclamation earlier this month, forcing civilians into military service […]
articles.context: The Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) has been at the center of a brutal conflict in Ethiopia since November 2020, when a military offensive was launched against it by the federal government. Following a fragile ceasefire in March 2022, tensions have resurfaced as the TPLF seeks to consolidate power in Tigray. The recent proclamation, introduced in October 2023, raises concerns about the potential resurgence of authoritarian practices in a region still recovering from extensive violence and humanitarian crises.

articles.title: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
articles.summary: Sixteen pupils, aged between 15 and 18, died when a fire broke out in a dormitory at Utumishi Girls' School last month.
articles.context: The tragic fire at Utumishi Girls' School on September 26, 2023, has reignited discussions about school safety and accountability in Kenya, particularly in the wake of ongoing debates about the management of educational institutions. The incident follows a series of school-related tragedies in the country, prompting the Ministry of Education to review safety regulations. The decision to charge the students with murder raises questions about juvenile justice and the broader implications for school governance in Kenya.

articles.title: Kenya signs $1.2B deal with Chinese firm to expand Nairobi airport
articles.summary: Project will nearly triple passenger capacity at East Africa's busiest aviation hub
articles.context: The deal comes amid Kenya's ongoing efforts to enhance its infrastructure as part of the Vision 2030 development plan, which aims to position Nairobi as a regional hub. The expansion is crucial following the COVID-19 pandemic's impact on air travel, with passenger numbers dropping significantly. Additionally, this partnership with a Chinese firm reflects Kenya's reliance on Chinese investment amid rising concerns over debt sustainability and geopolitical tensions in the region.

articles.title: Farmers urged to boost production as Aweil rice planting season begins
articles.summary: The National Minister of Agriculture has called on farmers in Northern Bahr el Ghazal State to take charge of rice production, saying the Aweil rice ccheme remains central to the government’s food security agenda. Speaking during the official launch of the Aweil Rice Scheme, Clement Juma Mbugoniwia said the project is a national priority under […] The post Farmers urged to boost production as Aweil rice planting season begins appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The Aweil Rice Scheme is part of South Sudan's broader efforts to enhance food security amid ongoing challenges from civil conflict and economic instability since the country's independence in 2011. The government, led by President Salva Kiir, has prioritized agricultural initiatives to reduce reliance on imports and address hunger, particularly following the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, which aimed to stabilize the region and promote development.

articles.title: EXPLAINER — Sudan war disrupts supplies of gum arabic, a hidden ingredient in everyday products
articles.summary: As fighting persists, Sudan’s globally important gum arabic industry faces mounting risks from insecurity and illicit trade
articles.context: Sudan is the world's largest producer of gum arabic, a key ingredient used in food, cosmetics, and pharmaceuticals. The ongoing conflict, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, has severely disrupted production and supply chains. This instability threatens not only local economies but also global markets that rely on Sudanese gum arabic, highlighting the urgent need for a ceasefire and humanitarian access amid the chaos.

articles.title: تكريم ياسمين عبد العزيز في بيفرلي هيلز تقديرًا لمسيرتها الفنية
articles.summary: تستعد النجمة ياسمين عبد العزيز لحصد تكريم جديد يضاف إلى سجل نجاحاتها، وذلك خلال احتفالية في مدينة Beverly Hills بولاية California اليوم الأربعاء، تقديرًا لمجمل أعمالها الفنية ومسيرتها الحافلة بالنجاحات في الدراما والسينما العربية. ويأتي هذا التكريم احتفاءً بالمكانة الكبيرة التي حققتها ياسمين عبد العزيز على مدار سنوات طويلة، بعدما نجحت في ترسيخ اسمها كواحدة …
articles.context: تأتي احتفالية تكريم ياسمين عبد العزيز في بيفرلي هيلز في وقت تشهد فيه المنطقة توترات سياسية واقتصادية، خاصة في السودان حيث تتفاقم الأزمات الإنسانية والنزاعات. يُعتبر تكريمها في الخارج بمثابة دعم للثقافة والفنون العربية في ظل الظروف الصعبة التي تواجهها العديد من الدول في المنطقة، مما يعكس أهمية الفن كوسيلة للتعبير والاحتفاء بالهوية الثقافية.

articles.title: وفاة عم الفنانة بسنت شوقى
articles.summary: خيمت حالة من الحزن على أسرة الفنانة بسنت شوقي بعد وفاة عمها طارق عبد الشافي، الذي رحل عن عالمنا اليوم الأربعاء، وسط حالة من الحزن بين أفراد العائلة والأصدقاء والمقربين. الراحل من عائلة رياضية عريقة ويُعد الراحل أحد أفراد عائلة عبد الشافي المعروفة رياضيًا، فهو شقيق الكابتن شوقي عبد الشافي، والد الفنانة بسنت شوقي، وكذلك …
articles.context: تأتي وفاة عم الفنانة بسنت شوقي في وقت تعاني فيه السودان من أزمات سياسية واقتصادية متزايدة، حيث تشهد البلاد صراعات داخلية منذ اندلاع النزاع في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع. تعكس هذه الأحداث تأثير الأزمات على الحياة الشخصية للفنانين والمشاهير، مما يزيد من شعور الحزن والفقد في المجتمع. عائلة عبد الشافي، المرتبطة بالرياضة والفن، تمثل جزءًا من النسيج الاجتماعي المتأثر بهذه الظروف.

articles.title: Session 91: Gatwech cites immunity, rejects “militia utterance” claim in Nasir case
articles.summary: The third accused in the ongoing Special Court trial over the Nasir Garrison attack, Hon. Gatwech Lam Pouch, has defended his public remarks and challenged electronic evidence presented by the prosecution. Continuing his defense testimony during the 91st court session on Wednesday, Gatwech told the court that he previously enjoyed parliamentary immunity, arguing that his […] The post Session 91: Gatwech cites immunity, rejects “militia utterance” claim in Nasir case appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The Nasir Garrison attack in 2013 was a pivotal event in South Sudan's ongoing civil conflict, which has seen various factions vying for power since the country’s independence in 2011. Hon. Gatwech Lam Pouch, a key figure from the SPLA-IO faction, is currently facing trial amid heightened tensions following the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, which aimed to establish lasting peace but has faced numerous implementation challenges.

articles.title: Authorities probe mystery cattle disease in Aweil East after 56 deaths
articles.summary: Authorities in Northern Bahr el Ghazal State are investigating a mysterious disease that has killed at least 56 cattle in Aweil East County. The State Ministry of Animal Resources and Fisheries says the cause of the outbreak has not yet been identified. According to the ministry, the affected animals are part of about 400 cattle […] The post Authorities probe mystery cattle disease in Aweil East after 56 deaths appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The outbreak comes amid ongoing tensions in South Sudan, particularly in Northern Bahr el Ghazal, where livestock is vital for the livelihoods of local communities. The region has faced recurrent conflicts over resources, exacerbated by the 2013 civil war and subsequent peace agreements, such as the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan (2018). This cattle disease could further destabilize the fragile security situation and threaten food security in the area.

articles.title: ECMA Approves Registration of Bunna Bank’s Securities
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) has finalised the registration of more than 64 million existing and new shares of Bunna Bank S.C, a public notice the regulatory
articles.context: The approval by the Ethiopian Capital Market Authority (ECMA) comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic reforms aimed at liberalizing the financial sector, initiated in 2018 under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. This move is significant as it reflects the government's efforts to stabilize the economy following the Tigray conflict, which has strained financial resources and investor confidence. The registration of Bunna Bank's shares is a crucial step in enhancing public participation in Ethiopia's emerging capital market.

articles.title: Ethiopia Selected as Africa’s Meteorology and Hydrology Training Center
articles.summary: It has been officially announced that the Ethiopian Meteorology Institute and Arba Minch University have been formally approved to serve as Regional Training Centers (RTC) for the World Meteorological Organization (WMO). This decision was passed during the 80th session of the World Meteorological Organization’s Executive Council meeting currently being held in Geneva, Switzerland. It was […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's selection as a Regional Training Center comes amid ongoing challenges, including the Tigray conflict that has strained the country's resources and international relations. The decision, made during the WMO's 80th Executive Council meeting in June 2023, highlights Ethiopia's commitment to enhancing regional climate resilience, particularly as the Horn of Africa grapples with severe droughts and climate change impacts. This approval also reflects a shift towards regional cooperation in addressing environmental challenges.

articles.title: Jonglei Governor criticizes aid agencies over ties to opposition areas, defends checkpoints
articles.summary: The Governor of Jonglei State, Dr. Riek Gai Kok, on Tuesday accused humanitarian organizations operating in opposition-controlled areas of engaging illegitimate authorities, and defended the use of checkpoints across the country, saying they are part of the state’s duties. Gai made the remarks during the opening of a Stakeholder Engagement Workshop on Humanitarian Access and […] The post Jonglei Governor criticizes aid agencies over ties to opposition areas, defends checkpoints appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: Jonglei State has been a focal point of conflict in South Sudan, particularly since the civil war erupted in December 2013, pitting the government against opposition forces led by figures like Riek Machar. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to stabilize the region, yet tensions remain high, especially regarding humanitarian access in opposition-controlled areas, complicating the delivery of aid amidst ongoing political rivalries.

articles.title: Ethiopia Highlights Benefits of New Africa-Wide Biotechnology Information Portal
articles.summary: Ethiopia is set to benefit from improved access to biotechnology and biosafety information following the launch of a new continental digital platform aimed at enhancing transparency and supporting evidence-based decision-making across Africa. The newly introduced Biotech Africa Database, developed by the International Service for the Acquisition of Agri-biotech Applications (ISAAA AfriCenter) and its partners, brings […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's launch of the Biotech Africa Database comes amid ongoing challenges in agricultural productivity exacerbated by climate change and regional conflicts, particularly the Tigray conflict that began in November 2020. With Ethiopia's economy heavily reliant on agriculture, this initiative, supported by ISAAA AfriCenter, aims to enhance food security and promote sustainable agricultural practices, crucial for stabilizing the region and fostering cooperation among neighboring countries like Sudan.

articles.title: Diplomat Kuol meets leaders of South Sudan Teachers’ Union in Egypt
articles.summary: Envoy Kuol Nyok Kuol Arop has met leaders of the South Sudan Teachers’ Union in Egypt in Cairo to review the state of South Sudanese educational centres and explore ways to improve their performance in line with Egyptian regulations. The discussions focused on strengthening education standards and addressing operational challenges facing South Sudanese schools in […] The post Diplomat Kuol meets leaders of South Sudan Teachers’ Union in Egypt appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The meeting comes amid ongoing efforts to stabilize South Sudan's education sector, which has been severely impacted by years of civil conflict since 2013. The revitalized peace agreement signed in 2018 aimed to promote national unity and development, yet educational infrastructure remains underfunded and poorly managed. Egypt's involvement reflects its broader strategy to enhance regional cooperation and support South Sudan's recovery, particularly in education, which is critical for building a peaceful future.

articles.title: South Sudan receives IGAD free movement protocol for ratification
articles.summary: South Sudan has officially received the IGAD Protocol on Free Movement of Persons from regional migration experts, marking a significant step toward the country’s ratification process. The Ministry of Justice and Constitutional Affairs confirmed receipt of the protocol, noting that South Sudan signed the agreement in June 2022, while its ratification has remained pending approval […] The post South Sudan receives IGAD free movement protocol for ratification appeared first on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The IGAD Protocol on Free Movement of Persons, signed by South Sudan in June 2022, aims to enhance regional integration and mobility amid ongoing challenges in governance and security. The ratification process is crucial as South Sudan navigates its post-civil war recovery, with the transitional government seeking to bolster relations with neighboring countries like Sudan and Ethiopia. articles.context: تتفاقم الأزمة السودانية منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني بقيادة الجنرال عبد الفتاح البرهان وقوات الدعم السريع بقيادة محمد حمدان دقلو (حميدتي). فشلت عدة محاولات دولية وإقليمية، مثل اتفاق جدة في يونيو 2023، في تحقيق تسوية دائمة. تأتي المبادرة السعودية الجديدة في سياق سعي الرياض لتعزيز نفوذها في المنطقة، خاصة بعد دورها في الوساطة بين الفرقاء في اليمن.

articles.context: تأتي تصريحات خالد عمر يوسف في سياق تصاعد النزاع في السودان منذ اندلاع الحرب الأهلية في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع. على الرغم من محاولات التوصل إلى اتفاقات لوقف إطلاق النار، مثل اتفاق جدة في يونيو 2023، إلا أن القتال استمر، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية وزيادة المخاوف من تصعيد أكبر في الصراع.

articles.title: المينا الهندي: الطائر “الرومانسي الشرير” الذي يهدّد البيئة والزراعة في دول عربية

articles.context: تزايدت التحذيرات بشأن المينا الهندي في ظل الأزمات البيئية والاقتصادية التي تواجهها دول مثل الأردن وليبيا ومصر، حيث تعاني هذه الدول من تأثيرات التغير المناخي والنزاعات المستمرة. في عام 2021، أُبرم اتفاق إقليمي لمكافحة التهديدات البيئية، لكن تكاثر المينا الهندي يهدد الجهود المبذولة لتحقيق الأمن الغذائي والاستقرار في المنطقة، مما يسلط الضوء على التحديات البيئية المتزايدة في سياق الصراعات السياسية والاقتصادية.

articles.title: “بريطانيا ليست عصية على الحكم… قادتها فقط لا يعرفون كيف يحكمون” – مقال في التلغراف
articles.summary: "بريطانيا ليست عصية على الحكم... قادتها فقط لا يعرفون كيف يحكمون" - مقال في التلغراف صدر الصورة، Getty Images التعليق على الصورة، كير ستارمر يعلن استقالته، في وقت يناقش مقال في "التلغراف" أزمة اختيار القادة داخل الأحزاب البريطانية. Published قبل ساعة واحدة مدة القراءة: 5 دقائق في جولة الصحف البريطانية اليوم، نتوقف عند ثلاثة مقالات The post “بريطانيا ليست عصية على الحكم… قادتها فقط لا يعرفون كيف يحكمون” – مقال في التلغراف appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأتي المقالة في وقت تعاني فيه المملكة المتحدة من أزمة سياسية متزايدة، حيث استقال كير ستارمر من زعامة حزب العمال في 2023 بعد سلسلة من الانتكاسات الانتخابية. هذا التوتر السياسي يتزامن مع تصاعد القضايا الاقتصادية والاجتماعية، مما يعكس عدم قدرة القادة على معالجة التحديات الملحة. في ظل هذه الظروف، تبرز الحاجة إلى قادة قادرين على تقديم حلول فعالة، وهو ما يتناوله المقال في "التلغراف".

articles.title: الأمم المتحدة: السودان لا يزال يشكل أكبر أزمة نزوح عالميًّا
articles.summary: قالت ممثلة المفوضية السامية للأمم المتحدة لشؤون اللاجئين بالسودان، ماري-هيلين فيرني، إن السودان ما زال يشكل أكبر أزمة نزوح عالمياً، في ظل فجوة تواجه تمويل أنشطة المفوضية تصل إلى 72%. وأضافت أن الاحتياجات الأكثر إلحاحاً هي متطلبات حقيقية لإنقاذ الحياة تتركز بشكل كبير في مناطق النزوح الجديد في دارفور وكردفان، وفقا لسودان تربيون.. The post الأمم المتحدة: السودان لا يزال يشكل أكبر أزمة نزوح عالميًّا appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تتواصل أزمة النزوح في السودان منذ اندلاع النزاع المسلح في دارفور عام 2003، حيث أدت الصراعات المستمرة بين الحكومة والجماعات المسلحة إلى تشريد الملايين. في عام 2021، تفاقمت الأوضاع مع تصاعد التوترات السياسية بعد الانقلاب العسكري، مما زاد من احتياجات النازحين. تظل المفوضية السامية للأمم المتحدة لشؤون اللاجئين تواجه تحديات كبيرة في تمويل جهود الإغاثة، مع وجود فجوة تمويلية تصل إلى 72%.

articles.title: «استغلال مقابل الإغاثة».. انتهاكات تعيد طرح الحل الغائب لحرب السودان
articles.summary: أعادت الانتهاكات التي كُشف عنها داخل مخيمات اللاجئين السودانيين في شرق تشاد تسليط الضوء على الكلفة الإنسانية الباهظة للحرب المستمرة في السودان. ومع استمرار الحرب التي اندلعت في السودان في أبريل/ نيسان 2023، تحولت بيئات النزوح واللجوء التي يفترض أن توفر الحماية، إلى مسرح لاتهامات خطيرة بالاستغلال والانتهاك. وبينما تتصاعد المطالب بالمحاسبة وتعزيز آليات الحماية … The post «استغلال مقابل الإغاثة».. انتهاكات تعيد طرح الحل الغائب لحرب السودان appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: اندلعت الحرب في السودان في أبريل 2023 بين القوات المسلحة السودانية وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى نزوح الملايين إلى دول الجوار، بما في ذلك تشاد. تاريخياً، كانت تشاد تستضيف اللاجئين السودانيين، لكن الوضع الحالي يعكس تفاقم الأزمات الإنسانية، حيث تتعرض الفئات الضعيفة للاستغلال في المخيمات. هذه الانتهاكات تبرز الحاجة الملحة للمحاسبة وتعزيز آليات الحماية الدولية وسط غياب الحلول السياسية المستدامة.

articles.title: من اليمن إلى إخوان السودان.. تكنولوجيا إيرانية قاتلة بوساطة حوثية
articles.summary: تتزايد المؤشرات على وجود دعم إيراني للجيش السوداني وحلفائه، بينهم جماعة الإخوان، عبر مسارات إمداد عسكري وتقنيات متطورة. هذا ما كشفت عنه شبكة “شيبا إنتليجنس” التي قالت إنها حصلت على معلومات من مصادر استخباراتية تشير إلى أن “جماعة الحوثي، بالتعاون مع شبكات تهريب عابرة للحدود، نقلت تكنولوجيا عسكرية إيرانية منخفضة التكلفة، لا سيما الصواريخ والطائرات … The post من اليمن إلى إخوان السودان.. تكنولوجيا إيرانية قاتلة بوساطة حوثية appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأتي هذه المعلومات في وقت حساس للسودان، حيث يشهد البلد صراعًا داخليًا متصاعدًا منذ اندلاع النزاع بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023. في ظل هذه الفوضى، تسعى جماعة الإخوان المسلمين إلى تعزيز نفوذها، مما يزيد من أهمية الدعم الإيراني المحتمل. كما أن العلاقات المتوترة بين إيران والدول العربية، خاصة بعد اتفاقات أبراهام في 2020، تجعل من هذه التطورات نقطة محورية في الصراع الإقليمي.

articles.title: سلطات بورتسودان وإثارة النعرات القبلية في الشرق.. بوابة جديدة للفوضى
articles.summary: تتصاعد المخاوف في شرق السودان من تنامي التوترات القبلية، وسط اتهامات لسلطات بورتسودان بتغذية الانقسامات الاجتماعية. وتحدثت وسائل إعلام محلية بينها موقع “سودان تريبيون” عن تحذيرات من استقطاب قبلي يشهده شرق السودان منذ أسابيع، وسط مخاوف من تصعيد الأوضاع مستقبلا. جاء ذلك على خلفية تصريحات مثيرة للجدل أطلقها ناظر عموم قبائل الهدندوة، رئيس المجلس الأعلى … The post سلطات بورتسودان وإثارة النعرات القبلية في الشرق.. بوابة جديدة للفوضى appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تاريخياً، شهد شرق السودان توترات قبلية متزايدة، خاصة بعد اندلاع النزاع في دارفور عام 2003، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الانقسامات الاجتماعية. في 2020، تم توقيع اتفاق سلام بين الحكومة السودانية وحركات مسلحة، لكن التوترات القبلية استمرت. تصريحات ناظر عموم قبائل الهدندوة، التي أثارت الجدل مؤخراً، تأتي في وقت حساس حيث تسعى السلطات إلى استقرار المنطقة وسط مخاوف من تصعيد النزاعات القبلية.

articles.title: Türkiye well placed to help reveal ‘truth’ about Sudan conflict, Sudanese official says
articles.summary: Amgad Fareid Eltayeb says Ankara’s ties with Muslim world, NATO countries give it special position on Sudan
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, has resulted in a humanitarian crisis and widespread displacement. Türkiye's historical ties with Sudan, particularly through military cooperation and economic investments, position it as a potential mediator. Additionally, Ankara's relationships with both the Muslim world and NATO allies could facilitate diplomatic efforts to address the conflict and promote stability in the region.

articles.title: تحذيرات أممية ودولية من أن تصبح الأبيض “فاشرًا” أخرى
articles.summary: أعرب مجلس الأمن التابع للأمم المتحدة عن قلقه إزاء التقارير التي تفيد بوجود تعزيزات عسكرية كبيرة من قبل قوات الدعم السريع حول مدينة الأبيض في السودان، محذرًا من خطر شن هجوم بري محتمل على المدينة. وأعرب أعضاء المجلس، في بيان صدر يوم السبت، عن قلقهم إزاء الخطر الوشيك لوقوع فظائع جماعية، وطالبوا قوات الدعم السريع … The post تحذيرات أممية ودولية من أن تصبح الأبيض “فاشرًا” أخرى appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأتي هذه التحذيرات في سياق تصاعد التوترات في السودان منذ اندلاع النزاع بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023. شهدت مدينة الأبيض، التي تعتبر مركزًا استراتيجيًا، تصعيدًا عسكريًا ملحوظًا، مما يثير مخاوف من تكرار أحداث فظائع جماعية مشابهة لتلك التي وقعت في مدينة فاشر خلال النزاع في دارفور. كما أن الوضع الإنساني في البلاد يزداد سوءًا مع تزايد النزوح والاحتياجات الأساسية.

articles.title: Exclusion from Investment Code Identified as Main Obstacle to Ethiopia’s Creative Industry
articles.summary: Stakeholders say the main obstacle facing Ethiopia’s cultural and creative industries is not a lack of government funding, but the sector’s exclusion from the national investment code. They argue that although the industry has strong potential to generate foreign exchange and create jobs, the absence of legal recognition within the country’s commercial framework has prevented […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's creative industry has historically been stifled by restrictive government policies and a lack of legal frameworks. The 2018 reforms under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed aimed to liberalize the economy, yet the exclusion of the cultural sector from the national investment code remains a significant barrier. This issue is particularly pressing as Ethiopia seeks to diversify its economy amidst ongoing ethnic tensions and the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which has strained resources and attention.

articles.title: “Resilience and Inclusion” Art Exhibition opens for World Refugee Day
articles.summary: An art exhibition themed “Resilience and Inclusion” opened today at the Modern Art Museum’s Gebrekristos Desta Center in Addis Ababa to mark World Refugee Day 2026. The group exhibition features works by seven refugee artists — five men and two women — from Eritrea, Somalia, Sudan, the Democratic Republic of Congo and Iraq. Through paintings […]
articles.context: The Horn of Africa has been grappling with significant humanitarian crises due to ongoing conflicts, particularly in Ethiopia's Tigray region since the civil war erupted in November 2020. This has led to a surge in refugees from neighboring countries like Eritrea and Sudan, exacerbating the region's instability. The art exhibition coincides with heightened global awareness of refugee issues, particularly following the 2021 signing of the Global Compact on Refugees, which emphasizes the need for inclusion and support for displaced populations.

articles.title: African bloc expresses ‘grave’ concern over escalating hostilities in Sudan's El Obeid city
articles.summary: Intergovernmental Authority on Development calls for immediate end to hostilities, all military operations
articles.context: El Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan, has seen a surge in violence amid the ongoing conflict between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, which escalated in April 2023. The Intergovernmental Authority on Development (IGAD) has been involved in peace negotiations, but recent clashes have undermined efforts to stabilize the region. The situation is further complicated by Sudan's historical ethnic tensions and the struggle for power among military factions.

articles.title: Matchday in the App Economy: How Football Fans Follow Odds and Stats in One Place
articles.summary: A football match used to live in a few places at once. The game was on television, the score was on a radio update, the league table was in the newspaper, and the argument about team selection happened somewhere else entirely. Now most of that sits on one screen. A fan can check the line-up, […]
articles.context: In Ethiopia, the rise of mobile technology and internet access has transformed how football fans engage with the sport, particularly following the 2020-2021 Ethiopian Premier League season, which saw increased viewership despite ongoing political unrest and conflict in the Tigray region. The Ethiopian Football Federation's efforts to promote the league and enhance fan engagement through digital platforms reflect a broader trend of leveraging technology in a country where traditional media has been fragmented.

articles.title: REQUEST FOR PROPOSALS FOR
articles.summary: REHABILITATION/RECONSTRUCTION OF WASH FACILITIES FOR 8 SCHOOLS IN THE TIGRAY REGION LRFP-2026-9204518 Topic- UNICEF (Ethiopia) wishes to request eligible bidders to participate in a Request for Proposal (LRFP) for Rehabilitation/Reconstruction of Wash Facilities For 8 Schools in The Tigray Region Details of this bid’s requirements and eligibility criteria can be found in the bid document. […]
articles.context: The Tigray region of Ethiopia has faced a devastating conflict since November 2020, leading to widespread humanitarian crises, including the destruction of infrastructure such as schools. The Ethiopian government and Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) reached a peace agreement in November 2022, yet the region continues to struggle with rebuilding essential services. UNICEF's initiative to rehabilitate WASH facilities in schools is crucial for restoring education and health in a region still recovering from the impacts of war.

articles.title: Board Announces Landslide Win for Prosperity Party
articles.summary: The incumbent Prosperity Party has won 438 out of a possible 486 parliamentary seats, the National Election Board of Ethiopia announced today. Opposition parties and individual candidates won just 48 seat, election officials disclosed at Addis Ababa’s Skylight Hotel, where President Taye Atskeselassie was also present. Only 501 of Parliament’s total 547 seats were in […]
articles.context: The Prosperity Party, formed in December 2018, emerged from the Ethiopian People's Revolutionary Democratic Front (EPRDF) and has faced significant challenges, including the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020. The recent elections, held amid ongoing tensions and a humanitarian crisis in Tigray, are crucial as they reflect the party's consolidation of power and the diminishing influence of opposition groups, particularly following the controversial 2021 elections that were marred by violence and allegations of fraud.

articles.title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
articles.summary: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.
articles.context: Abiy Ahmed's Prosperity Party secured a decisive victory in the June 2021 elections, despite ongoing ethnic tensions and violence in regions like Tigray, where a brutal civil war has persisted since November 2020. The conflict has drawn in neighboring countries, particularly Eritrea, complicating regional dynamics. Additionally, the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam dispute with Sudan and Egypt has heightened tensions, making the political stability of Ethiopia crucial for the Horn of Africa's security landscape.

articles.title: UN Security Council alarmed by paramilitary RSF surge around Sudan's El Obeid city
articles.summary: Council warns of 'imminent risk of mass atrocities' as capital of North Kordofan state faces potential ground offensive
articles.context: The Rapid Support Forces (RSF), a paramilitary group originally formed from the Janjaweed militias, have intensified their presence around El Obeid amid ongoing conflict following the Sudanese military coup in October 2021. The power struggle between the RSF and the Sudanese Armed Forces has escalated, particularly after the October 2022 ceasefire agreement failed to stabilize the region, raising fears of mass atrocities as the RSF seeks to expand its control in North Kordofan.

articles.title: KEFI subsidiary names CBE Capital to advise on Gold-Linked preference share issue
articles.summary: KEFI Gold and Copper Plc has announced that its subsidiary, KME Minerals (Ethiopia) Holding S.C., has appointed CBE Capital Investment Bank as adviser for a proposed issuance of U.S. dollar-denominated, gold-linked preference shares aimed at selected financial institutions in Ethiopia. The announcement was made at the Ethio-British Business Investment Forum in London. According to the […]
articles.context: KEFI Gold and Copper Plc's move comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing economic recovery following the Tigray conflict, which ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The Ethiopian government is actively seeking foreign investment to stabilize its economy, making this gold-linked preference share issue particularly significant. Additionally, the recent depreciation of the Ethiopian birr has heightened interest in dollar-denominated financial instruments, positioning KEFI's initiative as a strategic response to current economic challenges.

articles.title: Ethiopia Launches Groundbreaking “Makatet Roadmap”, Ushering in an Era of Refugee Inclusion
articles.summary: The Government of Ethiopia has officially launched the Makatet Roadmap in Addis Ababa, marking a historic turning point in its approach to refugee protection. Developed through an intense, nationally driven process involving federal and regional institutions, development partners, UN agencies, civil society and refugee representatives, this comprehensive framework established a unified blueprint for the socio-economic […]
articles.context: Ethiopia hosts over 800,000 refugees, primarily from South Sudan, Somalia, and Eritrea, amid ongoing conflicts and humanitarian crises in the region. The launch of the Makatet Roadmap comes after the 2020 Tigray conflict, which exacerbated displacement issues and strained resources. This initiative aligns with Ethiopia's commitments under the Global Compact on Refugees, aiming to enhance refugee inclusion and support amid a backdrop of political reforms and regional instability.

articles.title: 70% of Ethiopian households do not purchase toothpaste, new Worldpanel by Numerator data shows
articles.summary: New data from Worldpanel by Numerator suggests that oral hygiene products are not part of the regular shopping habits of many Ethiopian households, with around seven in ten households not purchasing toothpaste in the past year. The findings point to a significant gap in the adoption of one of the most widely recommended preventive oral […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant challenges in public health and access to basic hygiene products, exacerbated by the ongoing conflict in the Tigray region since November 2020. The humanitarian crisis has disrupted supply chains and limited access to essential goods, including oral hygiene products. Additionally, the government's focus on rebuilding and stabilizing the nation post-conflict highlights the urgent need for improved health education and access to preventive care, making these findings particularly relevant.

articles.title: ECA to host mid-year review to strengthen joint delivery and UN@80 reforms
articles.summary: The Economic Commission for Africa (ECA) is scheduled to hold its Second Quarter Accountability and Programme Performance Review Meeting (APPRM) at the United Nations Conference Centre in Addis Ababa from June 17 to 19. The event, themed “Enhancing Joint Delivery for Improved Results after UN@80 Reforms: A Mid-Year Review,” will bring together ECA’s leadership, programme […]
articles.context: The ECA's mid-year review comes at a critical time as Ethiopia navigates post-conflict recovery following the Tigray War, which officially ended with the Pretoria Agreement in November 2022. This meeting aims to address the urgent need for effective governance and economic stability in the region, particularly as the UN celebrates its 80th anniversary, emphasizing the importance of collaborative efforts in achieving sustainable development goals amidst ongoing challenges in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: ET Welcomes First Twin Otter Classic 300-G, eyes regional aviation stake expansion
articles.summary: De Havilland Aircraft, the Canadian aircraft maker, has delivered the first of two Twin Otter Classic 300-G aircraft to Ethiopian Airlines, as the national carrier embarks on improving its outreach to a regional market. The acquisition follows an agreement announced during the Paris Air Show in 2025 when Ethiopian Airlines confirmed plans to modernize and […]
articles.context: Ethiopian Airlines’ acquisition of the Twin Otter Classic 300-G comes amid Ethiopia's efforts to strengthen its regional influence following the Tigray conflict, which ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The airline aims to expand its services to neighboring countries like South Sudan and Somalia, enhancing connectivity and fostering economic ties in a region marked by political instability and a need for reliable transportation.

articles.title: TPLF Condemns US Visa Sanctions, Accuses Washington of One-Sided Approach
articles.summary: The Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) on Friday condemned newly imposed United States visa restrictions on some of its members, accusing Washington of taking a one-sided approach that overlooks what it described as the Ethiopian government’s failure to implement key provisions of the 2022 Pretoria peace agreement. In a statement issued a day after US […]
articles.context: The TPLF's condemnation follows the 2022 Pretoria peace agreement aimed at ending a brutal two-year conflict in Ethiopia's Tigray region, which resulted in significant humanitarian crises. Despite the agreement, tensions remain high as the TPLF accuses the Ethiopian government of not fulfilling its commitments, particularly regarding humanitarian access and political inclusion. The U.S. sanctions, imposed on October 20, 2023, are seen by the TPLF as an endorsement of the Ethiopian government's stance, exacerbating the political divide.

articles.title: TPLF Slams U.S. Visa Restrictions as “Imbalanced” and Selective
articles.summary: The Tigray People’s Liberation Front (TPLF) has strongly condemned the visa restrictions imposed on its members by the United States government, denouncing the decision as an “incomplete and imbalanced assessment” that places the entire blame for the region’s instability solely on the Tigrayan leadership. This diplomatic dispute erupted just a day after the U.S. Department […]
articles.context: The TPLF, once the dominant party in Ethiopia, has been embroiled in a brutal conflict with the federal government since November 2020, resulting in widespread humanitarian crises. The U.S. visa restrictions, announced on October 18, 2023, come amid ongoing tensions following the November 2022 peace agreement, which aimed to end hostilities but has seen continued instability in Tigray and surrounding regions. The TPLF's condemnation highlights its ongoing struggle for legitimacy and influence in Ethiopian politics.

articles.title: Borderland Frontiers: Bridges or Barriers?
articles.summary: Governance vacuums, cross-border insurgency, insecurity, human trafficking, arms supply, contraband trade alarm government Despite their deeply interconnected cultures and peoples, the countries of the Horn of Africa, like the rest of the continent, still bear the borders carved out for them by European colonists. Even though Ethiopia was never colonized, its boundaries were not immune […]
articles.context: The Horn of Africa has long been affected by colonial-era borders that ignore ethnic and cultural ties, leading to ongoing tensions. The 2020 Tigray conflict in Ethiopia exacerbated cross-border insurgencies, particularly involving the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) and Eritrean forces. Additionally, the 2022 peace agreement between the Ethiopian government and TPLF has not fully addressed the underlying issues of governance and security in border regions, making this a critical moment for regional stability.

articles.title: Federal Audit Pitfalls Continue, MPs Urge Better Budget Management
articles.summary: Outgoing members of Parliament put out yet another call for government officials to put a stop to the mismanagement of public resources this week. They did so after the Office of the Federal Auditor General (OFAG) presented its findings for the 2024/25 fiscal year to lawmakers on June 18, 2026. The audit covered 163 federal […]
articles.context: The call for improved budget management comes amid ongoing political tensions in Ethiopia, particularly following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which has strained federal resources and governance. The findings from the Office of the Federal Auditor General highlight systemic issues in public resource management, exacerbated by the government's focus on reconstruction efforts post-conflict. This scrutiny is crucial as Ethiopia navigates its recovery and seeks to restore public trust in its institutions.

articles.title: Arms Acquisition, TPLF’s New Alliances Risk Lasting Peace in Tigray: Foreign Minister
articles.summary: Speaking at a Chatham House event in London on June 17, 2026, Foreign Minister Gedion Timotheos warned that the hard-won peace in Ethiopia’s north is being actively jeopardized, underscoring the growing international and domestic anxiety about a return to conflict if core commitments to disarmament and regional cooperation are ignored. Gedion stated that hardliner elements […]
articles.context: The Tigray War, which erupted in November 2020 between the Ethiopian government and the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), officially ended with a peace agreement signed in Pretoria on November 2, 2022. Despite this, tensions remain high as the TPLF's recent arms acquisitions and shifting alliances raise concerns about the stability of the region. The Ethiopian government, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, faces pressure to uphold disarmament commitments to prevent a resurgence of violence.

articles.title: EU Calls on Eritrea to Investigate Wartime Rights Violations
articles.summary: The European Union has called on the Eritrean government to investigate international human rights violations committed by its military during and after the two-year war in northern Ethiopia. “In line with OHCHR recommendations, we call on the government of Eritrea to carry out credible, independent investigations into alleged violations and abuses of human rights and violations […]
articles.context: The call for investigations comes in the wake of the Tigray War (November 2020 - November 2022), where Eritrean forces were accused of committing atrocities alongside Ethiopian troops against Tigrayan civilians. The conflict ended with the Pretoria Agreement in November 2022, yet reports of human rights abuses persist, prompting the EU's demand for accountability. The ongoing tensions between Eritrea and Ethiopia, coupled with Eritrea's repressive domestic policies, further complicate the region's stability.

articles.title: The Critical Imperative to Avert a Second Civil War
articles.summary: Northern Ethiopia stands once again at a terrifying and existential crossroads. Less than four years after the November 2022 signing of the Pretoria Cessation of Hostilities Agreement, which brought a fragile end to one of the deadliest conflicts of the twenty-first century, the drums of war are beating with renewed, deafening intensity. Recent developments indicate […]
articles.context: The Tigray War, which erupted in November 2020, resulted in significant loss of life and displacement, culminating in the Pretoria Cessation of Hostilities Agreement signed on November 2, 2022. Despite this fragile peace, tensions have escalated in Northern Ethiopia, particularly involving the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) and the Ethiopian federal government. Recent military mobilizations and political maneuvers threaten to unravel the hard-won gains from the agreement, raising fears of renewed conflict.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s Hydrogen Ambitions Face Financing Hurdle as Africa’s Projects Stall: IEA
articles.summary: Africa’s plans to become a global supplier of low-emissions hydrogen are struggling to move beyond the drawing board, with only one of the 31 projects announced across the continent for deployment by 2030 securing a Final Investment Decision (FID), according to the International Energy Agency’s (IEA) Global Hydrogen Review 2026. The report paints a sobering […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's ambitions for hydrogen production come amid ongoing challenges, including the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which has strained the country's economy and infrastructure. The Ethiopian government has been actively pursuing renewable energy projects, particularly since the 2019 signing of the Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam agreement, but the lack of financial backing for hydrogen initiatives highlights the broader regional struggle for investment in clean energy amid political instability and economic pressures.

articles.title: Oilseed Exporters Decry New Tax Procedures, Warn of Imminent Liquidity Crisis
articles.summary: Nearly a third of Ethiopia’s pulses and oilseeds exporters are considering shutting down operations following the implementation of new income tax provisions, according to an assessment conducted by an industry lobby group, which warns the conditions could deepen liquidity pressures in one of the country’s key foreign exchange-earning industries. The study, conducted by the Ethiopian […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's oilseed sector has been a crucial part of its economy, contributing significantly to foreign exchange earnings, especially since the 2018 reforms aimed at liberalizing the agricultural market. However, ongoing political tensions and conflict in the Tigray region, which escalated in November 2020, have strained supply chains and investor confidence. The recent introduction of stringent tax measures in 2023 threatens to exacerbate financial instability for exporters already grappling with these challenges.

articles.title: Watchdog Spotlights Conflict’s Heavy Toll on Ethiopian Education
articles.summary: A New York-based watchdog ranks Ethiopia among the countries with the highest incidence of attacks on education worldwide alongside Haiti, Palestine, Ukraine, and the Democratic Republic of Congo. Global Coalition to Protect Education from Attack, an organization formed in 2010 and working closely with Amnesty International, Human Rights Watch, and UNICEF, identifies Ethiopia as one […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been embroiled in a multifaceted conflict since the Tigray War erupted in November 2020, leading to widespread violence and displacement. The ongoing tensions between the federal government and various regional factions, particularly in Tigray, Amhara, and Oromia, have severely disrupted educational institutions. The 2022 peace agreement between the federal government and Tigray forces has not fully alleviated the crisis, as attacks on schools continue amidst ongoing ethnic strife and political instability.

articles.title: Ethiopia, US Top Hargeisa’s Priority List as President Abdullahi Confirms Open Diplomatic Channels
articles.summary: Somaliland President Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi (Irro) says his administration is maintaining open diplomatic channels with the United States and Ethiopia in a bid to secure further international recognition. He spoke about these priorities during an interview with television network i24NEWS in Jerusalem on June 18, 2026. Irro’s official visit follows Israel’s formal decision to recognize […]
articles.context: Somaliland, which declared independence from Somalia in 1991, has sought international recognition but remains unrecognized by the UN. The recent normalization of relations between Israel and several Arab states has shifted regional dynamics, prompting Somaliland to strengthen ties with the U.S. and Ethiopia, both key players in the Horn of Africa. President Irro's outreach follows Ethiopia's ongoing federal challenges and the U.S.'s strategic interests in stabilizing the region amid rising tensions in Somalia.

articles.title: Empowering Ethiopian Health: Sustainable Models and Domestic Financing
articles.summary: For more than three decades, MSI Ethiopia has stood at the forefront of advancing reproductive healthcare and family planning across the nation. Dr. Abebe Shibru, country director for MSI Ethiopia Reproductive Choices (formerly Marie Stopes International Ethiopia), spoke with The Reporter’s Sisay Sahlu about his leadership and the organisation’s strategic shift amidst global financial cuts […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has faced significant challenges in healthcare due to ongoing political instability and conflict, particularly following the Tigray War that erupted in November 2020. The conflict has strained resources and disrupted healthcare services, making organizations like MSI Ethiopia crucial for reproductive health. Additionally, the recent global financial cuts to NGOs have prompted a strategic shift towards sustainable domestic financing models, highlighting the need for resilience in the face of these challenges.

articles.title: ESX Equity Market surpasses 1Billion Birr; A New Company is Set to Join the Market Next Week
articles.summary: The Ethiopian Securities Exchange (ESX) has achieved a new historic milestone by pushing its weekly equity trading volume past 1 billion birr for the first time, a feat stated to reflect the growing participation and confidence of investors in the country’s capital market. ESX Chief Executive Officer Tilahun Esmael Kassahun noted that for Ethiopia’s young […]
articles.context: The Ethiopian Securities Exchange (ESX) has emerged as a critical component of Ethiopia's economic reforms following the 2018 political transition led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed. This shift aimed to liberalize the economy amidst ongoing ethnic tensions and conflict, particularly in the Tigray region. The recent surge in trading volume reflects increased investor confidence, crucial for attracting foreign investment and stabilizing the economy as the country navigates these challenges.

articles.title: Addis Ababa City’s Budget Proposal for the Upcoming Year Tops Half a Trillion Birr, Showing a 43% Increase from Last Year
articles.summary: The Cabinet of the Addis Ababa City Administration, in its 5th year 12th regular meeting, has approved the city’s draft budget of 502.27 billion birr (over half a trillion birr) for the 2026/27 fiscal year and directed it to the City Council. This proposed budget shows a significant increase of 152.14 billion birr, or 43.45 […]
articles.context: The proposed budget comes amid Ethiopia's ongoing recovery from a two-year civil conflict that significantly impacted the economy and public services.
